channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: NASA declares its Mars Maven spacecraft dead after 6 months of silence - NBC News
item.description:
1. NASA declares its Mars Maven spacecraft dead after 6 months of silence  NBC News
  2. NASA Says Farewell to MAVEN Mars Mission, Hosts Media Call Today  NASA (.gov)
  3. NASA declares its Mars Maven spacecraft dead after six months of silence  ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
  4. NASA's MAVEN Mars orbiter is officially dead after months of radio silence  Space
  5. 'In an unrecoverable state': NASA confirms MAVEN spacecraft is officially dead after orbital 'anomaly' behind Mars  Live Science

item.description:

  4. A secret to making a queen bee may lie in the wax around it  Science News
  5. Queen bees emerge from special wax chambers  Chemical & Engineering News

item.description:

item.title: Mars was once a warmer world of rivers, lakes and a thicker atmosphere, but after its internal dynamo died and the planet lost the magnetic shield that helps protect an atmosphere, the solar wind stripped much of its air away over billions of years, leaving the c - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Mars was once a warmer world of rivers, lakes and a thicker atmosphere, but after its internal dynamo died and the planet lost the magnetic shield that helps protect an atmosphere, the solar wind stripped much of its air away over billions of years, leaving the c  Space Daily
  2. NASA Uses Mineralogical Marker to Understand Ancient Martian Climate  NASA Science (.gov)
  3. Mars Hid its Warm, Wet Crystals Underground  Universe Today
  4. Carbon-Rich Rocks May Have Cooled the Ancient Martian Atmosphere  eos.org
  5. Mars’s Gale Crater Would Have Been Habitable For Millions Of Years  Orbital Today

item.title: A tongue-eating louse called Cymothoa exigua swims into a fish's gills, latches onto its tongue, drinks the blood until the tongue withers and falls off, and then spends the rest of its life acting as a functional replacement tongue the fish uses normally to eat. - Space Daily
item.description: A tongue-eating louse called Cymothoa exigua swims into a fish's gills, latches onto its tongue, drinks the blood until the tongue withers and falls off, and then spends the rest of its life acting as a functional replacement tongue the fish uses normally to eat.  Space Daily

item.title: The light from the Andromeda Galaxy — the most distant object visible to the naked human eye — left its source about 2.5 million years ago, which means when you look at it on a clear night, you are seeing light that began its journey to Earth around the time th - Space Daily
item.description: The light from the Andromeda Galaxy — the most distant object visible to the naked human eye — left its source about 2.5 million years ago, which means when you look at it on a clear night, you are seeing light that began its journey to Earth around the time th  Space Daily

item.description:

item.title: Almost all the gold on Earth — every wedding ring, every coin, every gram in every bank vault — was forged in the collision of dead stars billions of years ago, in events so violent that a single one can produce hundreds of Earth-masses of gold, and the heavy - Space Daily

item.title: There is a planet 63 light-years from Earth where the rain is made of molten glass, the winds blow at 7,000 kilometres per hour, the daytime temperature is over 1,000 degrees Celsius, and the planet itself, viewed from space, is the same deep blue as Earth. - Space Daily
item.description: There is a planet 63 light-years from Earth where the rain is made of molten glass, the winds blow at 7,000 kilometres per hour, the daytime temperature is over 1,000 degrees Celsius, and the planet itself, viewed from space, is the same deep blue as Earth.  Space Daily

item.title: More than 60% of the water in a wood frog's body can freeze solid each winter: its heart stops, it stops breathing, and for more than 7 months it can lie essentially a frogsicle, before it thaws out in spring and simply hops away - Space Daily
item.description: More than 60% of the water in a wood frog's body can freeze solid each winter: its heart stops, it stops breathing, and for more than 7 months it can lie essentially a frogsicle, before it thaws out in spring and simply hops away  Space Daily

item.title: Parts of Canada are quietly short on gravity. The standard story blames an ice sheet that pressed the crust down and vanished thousands of years ago, but satellites suggest that explains less than half of it. The rest comes from something churning far deeper in - Space Daily
item.description: Parts of Canada are quietly short on gravity. The standard story blames an ice sheet that pressed the crust down and vanished thousands of years ago, but satellites suggest that explains less than half of it. The rest comes from something churning far deeper in  Space Daily

item.title: Scientists confirm a deep earthquake that shouldn't exist - ScienceDaily
item.description:
1. Scientists confirm a deep earthquake that shouldn't exist  ScienceDaily
  2. Scientists Finally Figured Out Why Strange Earthquakes Have Been Hitting Utah for Decades  VICE
  3. Geologists Keep Detecting Earthquakes Deep Below Utah That Nobody Can Feel  IFLScience
  4. You Didn't Feel Continental Mantle Earthquakes, But They Happened. A Lot  Science 2.0
  5. “How in the World Can These Things Happen?”: After a Series of ‘Mystery’ Quakes Shook Utah, Scientists Finally Think They Know Why  The Debrief

item.title: One of the earliest great extinctions in Earth's history may have been caused not by an asteroid or a volcano but by oxygen itself, when tiny photosynthetic microbes slowly filled the air with a gas that was poison to much of the anaerobic life that ruled the planet - Space Daily
item.description:
1. One of the earliest great extinctions in Earth's history may have been caused not by an asteroid or a volcano but by oxygen itself, when tiny photosynthetic microbes slowly filled the air with a gas that was poison to much of the anaerobic life that ruled the planet  Space Daily
  2. Want an oxygen-rich atmosphere? Stuff oxygen’s friends in the mantle.  Ars Technica
  3. Earth's oxygen-rich atmosphere may owe its existence to cold subduction  Phys.org
  4. The oxygen in Earth's atmosphere is essential to almost every animal alive today — but when it first started accumulating in the air roughly 2.4 billion years ago, it triggered the most lethal pollution event in the planet's history, wiping out the vast majority of spec  Space Daily
